tldr: Visma | Lease a Bike won the 28.4 km team time trial, beating Netcompany–INEOS by 9 seconds; Alex Baudin kept the yellow jersey as the TTT redrew the GC behind him.

narrative.headline: Visma win the team time trial
narrative.body: Over the 28.4 km Perreux loop, Netcompany–INEOS led at both intermediate splits (Coutievre and Montagny), but Visma | Lease a Bike turned the screw in the closing kilometres to win by nine seconds. EF Education–EasyPost took third at +0:29, protecting Alex Baudin's overall lead, while Lidl–Trek (+0:32) and Red Bull–BORA–hansgrohe (+0:40) kept their GC men in contention. UAE Team Emirates lost over a minute as a team (+1:01), dropping Isaac del Toro down the early GC.
narrative.gcConsequence: Baudin kept yellow with EF's strong ride, but the gaps between GC teams here would matter for the eventual podium.

gcImpact.headline: Baudin holds yellow; the TTT separates the GC men
gcImpact.body: Alex Baudin retained the lead. Behind him the GC reshuffled along team lines: Kévin Vauquelin (+0:12) and Oscar Onley moved up via INEOS's strong TTT, with Matteo Jorgenson (+0:15) close behind for Visma. Juan Ayuso and Mattias Skjelmose sat at +0:47 for Lidl–Trek, while Isaac del Toro was already +1:16 down after UAE's slower team ride — a deficit that framed his eventual late-race comeback.

riderPerformances.name: Matteo Jorgenson
riderPerformances.narrative: Jorgenson's Visma squad won the team time trial, lifting him to the fringe of the GC top three at +0:15. The strong TTT was the foundation of his week, keeping him in podium contention until the final climbs.

riderPerformances.name: Kévin Vauquelin
riderPerformances.narrative: Netcompany–INEOS led the TTT at both checkpoints before fading to second by nine seconds. The ride vaulted Vauquelin to second on GC at +0:12, his best position of the race.

riderPerformances.name: Juan Ayuso
riderPerformances.narrative: Lidl–Trek's solid fourth-place team ride kept Ayuso and Skjelmose at +0:47, a manageable deficit going into the mountains.

riderPerformances.name: Isaac del Toro
riderPerformances.narrative: UAE Team Emirates' modest TTT (+1:01 as a team, ninth) left del Toro +1:16 down on GC — the worst-placed of the genuine favourites. Recovering that minute-plus would define his fight back over the final two mountain days.
